title: Ghana vs. Panama: World Cup score, highlights of Group L match
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(html:paragraphs) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
June 17, 2026; Toronto, Canada; Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi celebrates scoring their first goal with Christopher Baah and Alidu Seidu.

After a back-and-forth match, Ghana had the final say and beat Panama 1-0 in Group L play in the 2026 World Cup. The game was played on Wednesday, June 17 at BMO Field in Toronto.

Caleb Yirenkyi finally got a goal in the 95th minute of the match. He scored right in front of the net off an assist from Brandon Thomas-Asante, who received the ball from deep across the pitch.

When the final whistle blew, manager Carlos Queiroz, who took the reins of the Black Stars only two months before the World Cup kickoff, ran across the pitch to celebrate with Ghana fans.

Panama had several early chances to score and kept Black Stars goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi busy. It was a physical match where Ati-Zigi received medical attention twice in the first half. He was replaced by Benjamin Asare to start the second half.

This was Panama’s second-ever World Cup appearance after qualifying for the first time in 2018. Ghana has been here five times before, including the 2022 World Cup, but are dealing with the controversial absence of midfielder Thomas Partey, who was denied entry to the United States due to allegations of sexual assault in London.

Ghana gets three points for the win in Group L.

The match is over! Caleb Yirenkyi's late goal with an assist from Brandon Thomas-Asante was the only score, giving Ghana the 1-0 win over Panama to open Group L play.

90' + 9' Yellow Card! Carlos Harvey handed a penalty

Panama's Carlos Harvey was given a yellow card at the end of a long, physical match.

Édgar Yoel Bárcenas was given a corner kick late in the game. He lined up and smacked the ball onto the pitch where Panama put all 11 players toward the goal. Goalkeeper Orlando Mosquera attempted the header, but Black Stars goalkeeper Benjamin Asare snatched the ball. Asare collapsed on the pitch and kicked his legs, seemingly in pain, while several players started to bicker among themselves. Referees and teammates on both sides attempted to break everything up.

90' + 5' GOAL! Caleb Yirenky puts Ghana up 1-0 over Panama

Brandon Thomas-Asante tapped the ball to the center of the pitch to Caleb Yirenkyi, who slapped it into the net for the first goal of the match. BMO Field erupted at finally getting a goal in a back-and-forth game.

6 minutes of stoppage time added to second half of Ghana vs. Panama

Officials added six minutes of stoppage time to the second half of the Group L match between Ghana and Panama. The score is 0-0.

Brandon Thomas-Asante got very close to scoring for Ghana. He dribbled up close to the net and smacked the ball, but Panama goalkeeper Orlando Mosquera dove in front of it. Thomas-Asante was ruled offsides anyways.

Ghana and Panama go back and forth heading toward the end of the match. Despite several shots, nothing is going into the net.

The match is still scoreless between Panama and Ghana at the hydration break of the second half.

Ghana nearly scores, but comes up just short. On a fast break play, Fatawu Issahaku received a pass, stutter stepped in front of the goal and tapped the ball. It rolled just to the left of the net.

Cristian Martínez nearly scored a goal for Panama. He received the ball and dribbled to his right and then smacked it across his body toward the net at his left. Gideon Mensah slid in front of him to block the shot and the ball flew away behind the goal.

José Luis Rodríguez fell to the pitch after running into a Ghana player. He held his leg and grimaced, but got up and continued to play.

José Luis Rodríguez attempted a corner kick for Panama. He boosted the ball onto the pitch and it was easily met by Ghana goalkeeper Benjamin Asare.

Ghana's starting goalkeeper, Lawrence Ati-Zigi, was beaten up during the first half of the match against Panama. He notched one save and got tangled up with players several times, receiving medical attention twice.

Manager Carlos Queiroz opted to take Ati-Zigi out and replace him with Benjamin Asare to start the second half.

The swap counts as one of the Black Stars' five substitutions.

At the end of the first half, the match between Panama and Ghana is scoreless. Panama has had several shots, but Lawrence Ati-Zigi has one save and has put his body on the line several times to ensure the South American side did not score.

5 minutes of stoppage time added to first half of Ghana vs. Panama

Officials have added five minutes to the end of the first half. The match is scoreless.

Ghana goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi has had an intense first half. He was knocked down again toward the end of the half when he went to defend a goal and his legs were swept out from underneath him. Medical staff tended to him for the second time this match.

After a series of physical plays, including one where Panama called for a push, two Ghana players were injured. In a second play, Panama's Andrés Andrade kicked the ball toward the goal and goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi and a few other players got tangled up, including Jerome Opoku, who fell to the ground. After lying on his side for a few seconds, Opoku got up off the pitch, but team doctors tended to Ati-Zigi.

At the hydration break midway through the first half, Panama and Ghana are scoreless. The South American side has had a few shots toward the goal. Lawrence Ati-Zigi had a great save for Ghana.

16' Yellow Card! Caleb Yirenkyi handed a penalty

Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi was handed a yellow card in what is turning out to be a physical match.

Cecilio Waterman received a pass at the center of the field at the top of the penalty box and smacked the ball toward the goal. Ghana goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi expertly dove to swat the ball away.

What jerseys are Panama and Ghana wearing in World Cup opening match?

Panama is wearing their home shirt by Reebok with a red base and navy collar.

Ghana is wearing their yellow Puma away kits.

Panama and Ghana's Group L match is underway in Toronto with a light drizzle of rain.

Ghana and Panama will kick off their 2026 World Cup opener at 7 p.m. ET Wednesday, June 17.

The game is airing on Fox, but you can also watch on Fubo or the Fox One App . Spanish language broadcasts are available on Telemundo and Peacock .

Carlos Queiroz was hired by Ghana just two months before the World Cup kickoff. The 73-year-old has experience in the Premier League with Manchester United under Sir Alex Ferguson and helmed La Liga powerhouse Real Madrid. He has coached five World Cup teams, leading South Africa and Portugal for a tournament each and three competitions with Iran. The farthest he's made it is the Round of 16 with Portugal and Cristiano Ronaldo when they outlasted that year's Group of Death with Brazil, Ivory Coast and North Korea, who they handled 7-0 in the group stage.

Panama's manager is Thomas Christiansen, who has had the reins since 2020. The former Leeds United boss took the team to the Nations League semifinals in 2023 and 2024 and to the final in 2025. He has found success giving newer players confidence while empowering veterans too.

Where are Panama and Ghana's World Cup training camps?

Panama's World Cup training camp is at Nottawasaga Resort in Alliston, Ontario.

Ghana's training camp for the tournament is at Bryant University in Smithfield, Rhode Island.

See the full list of World Cup training camps here.

Panama fans are ready for action in Toronto! A large group of supporters gathered at Coronation Park just across the street from BMO Field ahead of the team's Group L match against Ghana.

Ghana is in Group L in the 2026 World Cup, for their fifth time on the global platform. Here is the country's history at the tournament:

Panama is in Group L in the 2026 World Cup, for only their second time on the global platform. Here is the country's history at the tournament:
